mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals a supply-side shock that compresses housing market prices and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in > 70% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a supply-side shock that compresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that boosts discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening monetary cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month decline in payroll employment signals a weakening labor market that boosts unemployment and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to produce negative returns in > 70% of instances.
